anzsic_codes.name: Management Advice and Related Consulting Services

merger_description:
SLR Consulting Australia Pty Ltd (**SLR**) proposes to acquire:

* 100% of the issued share capital of Hydrobiology Pty Ltd (**Hydrobiology AU**),
* the 33% of the issued share capital of Hydrobiology WA Pty Ltd (**Hydrobiology WA**) not owned by Hydrobiology AU, and
* the 33% of the issued share capital of Hydrobiology Singapore Pte Ltd (**Hydrobiology Singapore**) not owned by Hydrobiology AU,

(the **Acquisition**). As a result of its acquisition of Hydrobiology AU, SLR will also indirectly acquire Hydrobiology Management Pty Ltd and Hydrobiology QLD Pty Ltd, as well as the balance of the issued share capital of Hydrobiology WA and Hydrobiology Singapore owned by Hydrobiology AU.

SLR (and its subsidiaries) supply environmental consulting services in Australia, including strategic advisory and technical consulting to its clients across the energy, finance, mining, infrastructure, manufacturing, and technology industries. The environmental consulting services SLR supplies include ESG advisory services, environmental risk assessments, impact assessments, engineering, scientific and technical services, ecological, efficient energy and water management services, and town planning and project management services.

Hydrobiology AU and its subsidiaries (together, **Hydrobiology**) supply integrated environmental consulting services in the physical, biological and chemical processes of environmental systems, with a particular emphasis on aquatic environments or ecosystems, generally referred to as ‘aquatic environments consulting services’. Hydrobiology has offices in Queensland, Western Australia, and Singapore, and has 54 staff in Australia. Its core focuses are environmental risk assessments, impact assessments and environmental management, for projects affecting aquatic environments or ecosystems consisting of marine, freshwater and coastal systems.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
SLR Consulting Australia Pty Ltd (SLR) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:
a. 100% of the issued share capital of Hydrobiology Pty Ltd
(Hydrobiology AU),
b. the 33% of the issued share capital of Hydrobiology WA Pty
Ltd not owned by Hydrobiology AU, and
c. the 33% of the issued share capital of Hydrobiology
Singapore Pte Ltd not owned by Hydrobiology AU,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, SLR (together with its subsidiaries), supplies
environmental consulting services in Australia, including strategic
advisory and technical consulting to its clients across the energy,
finance, mining, infrastructure, manufacturing, and technology
industries. The environmental consulting services SLR supplies
include ESG advisory services, environmental risk assessments,
impact assessments, engineering, scientific and technical services,
ecological, efficient energy and water management services, and
town planning and project management services.
The target, Hydrobiology AU (together with its subsidiaries,
Hydrobiology), supplies integrated environmental consulting
services in the physical, biological, and chemical processes of
environmental systems, with a particular emphasis on aquatic
environments or ecosystems, generally referred to as ‘aquatic
environments consulting services’. Hydrobiology has offices in
Queensland, Western Australia, and Singapore, and has 54 staff in
Australia. Its core focuses are environmental risk assessments,
impact assessments, and environmental management, for projects
affecting aquatic environments or ecosystems consisting of marine,
freshwater, and coastal systems.
Both SLR and Hydrobiology provide environmental consulting
services, including for aquatic environments, in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is limited overlap between the types of environmental
consulting services offered by the parties, as SLR is a
multidisciplinary environmental consulting firm and
Hydrobiology specialises in the supply of aquatic
environments consulting services
b. the parties’ combined shares of supply of environmental
consulting services and aquatic environments consulting
services nationally are estimated to be low
c. there are alternative providers of environmental consulting
services and aquatic environments consulting services in
Australia.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
